channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lok Sabha suspends TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee for remainder of Monsoon Session over unparliamentary remarks
item.description: The Lok Sabha on Wednesday passed a resolution suspending Trinamool Congress (TMC) Member of Parliament Kalyan Banerjee for the remainder of the Monsoon Session after he allegedly used unparliamentary...

item.title: Synthesia Expands AI Training Platform to Include Live Coaching and Performance Analytics
item.description: Synthesia, a British AI startup, has launched Roleplay Sessions, an interactive training product that allows employees to practice high-stakes conversations like sales pitches, performance reviews, an...

item.title: Nairobi County Cuts Legal Costs by Boosting Internal Legal Team
item.description: Nairobi City County has saved an estimated Sh8.48 billion over the last two financial years by reducing reliance on external law firms and strengthening its in-house legal department. The County Assem...

item.title: Tanzania Announces Economic Measures and Anti-Trafficking Efforts
item.description: The article highlights several key developments in Tanzania. First, there are indications of trade peaks preceding a potential quarterly economic slowdown, prompting concerns about market stability. A...

item.title: Discovery of Pan Am crash wreckage underscores historical advancements in flight safety
item.description: The wreckage of a Pan Am DC-4 aircraft that crashed in the ocean in 1952 has been discovered off Puerto Rico's coast, marking a significant moment in aviation history. This crash, which claimed 52 liv...

item.title: Canadian Boycotts California Wine Amid Trade Tensions with US
item.description: The article features letters from Canadian readers expressing frustration over ongoing trade disputes with the United States. One writer, a Canadian citizen, declares they will never purchase Californ...

item.title: I Belong To The Zoo to Host First Major Concert with Ticket Information Released
item.description: The article announces the release of ticket details for I Belong To The Zoo's first major concert, marking a significant milestone for the group. The event is set to be a major entertainment spectacle...

item.title: NNPP Chairman Accuses Kwankwaso Of Using Party As Bargaining Chip
item.description: The National Chairman of the New Nigerian Peoples Party (NNPP), Agbo Gilbert Major, has accused former Kano State Governor Rabiu Kwankwaso of using the party as a bargaining chip during the 2023 gener...

item.title: Fuel Price Surge Sparks Two-Year High Inflation in South Africa
item.description: South Africa's annual consumer inflation surged to 5% in June, marking the highest level since June 2024. This increase is primarily attributed to a sharp rise in fuel prices, which have climbed 34.3%...

item.title: Philippines Conducts Medical Evacuation from Ayungin Without China's Permission
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) clarified that China did not need to grant permission for the medical evacuation of two injured Filipino servicemen from Ayungin Shoal, as the operation was conducted ...

item.title: South Sydney's Injury Crisis and NRL 2026 Challenges
item.description: The article details the ongoing challenges faced by South Sydney Rabbitohs in the 2026 NRL season, with key players sidelined due to injuries. Latrell Mitchell, the team's star forward, is dealing wit...

item.title: Tunduma Youth Navigate Loan Challenges and Systemic Barriers
item.description: The article highlights the struggles of young people in Tunduma, Tanzania, who face systemic challenges in accessing government loans and are forced to turn to predatory lenders. Despite bureaucratic ...

item.title: Military Operations in Balochistan Target Terrorists and Suicide Bombers
item.description: Security forces conducted operations in Balochistan's Surab and Mastung districts over the past 48 hours, killing 10 terrorists and apprehending two female suicide bombers along with their facilitator...

item.title: Jigawa Governor Inspects State Secretariat Amid Absenteeism Concerns
item.description: Jigawa State Governor Umar Namadi conducted an unannounced inspection of the state secretariat in Dutse, discovering several offices locked and empty during working hours. The governor expressed disap...

item.title: Ethiopian Police Arrest Suspects in Major Human Trafficking Ring
item.description: Ethiopian Federal Police have arrested 17 suspects, including the alleged leader of a multi-million-dollar international human trafficking network. The gang is accused of orchestrating the deaths of 6...

item.title: Calgary Man Attempted to Hit Pedestrians in Northern Ontario, Set Belongings on Fire
item.description: A Calgary man was arrested after attempting to strike pedestrians in northern Ontario and setting his belongings ablaze. According to police reports, the incident occurred in a populated area, prompti...

item.title: Wes Streeting apologizes for comments on early prisoner release scheme
item.description: Wes Streeting, the UK's new defence secretary, has apologized for comments made during a private conversation that were later captured on audio, referencing the government's early prisoner release sch...

item.title: US and China Discuss Regional Security Amid Asean Summit
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi during an Association of Southeast Asian Nations (Asean) summit, where diplomats addressed tensions in the Middle East and South ...

item.title: Assam Floods Claim 21 Lives, Over 5.6 Lakh Affected as Rescue Operations Continue
item.description: Floods in Assam have claimed 21 more lives, bringing the death toll to 31 in recent days. Over 5.6 lakh people across 16 districts have been impacted, with Sivasagar being the worst-affected area. The...

item.title: DCP Condemns Jubilee for Contradictory Stance on Tourism and Economic Policies
item.description: The Democracy for Citizens Party (DCP) has criticized the Jubilee Party for its inconsistent approach to political unity and economic management. DCP secretary general John Methu accused Jubilee of hy...
